_comment: OneIM VB.NET Auditor configuration. Values here override built-in defaults. All keys are optional; missing keys fall back to the built-in default shown in each comment.

detection._comment_header_scan_lines: Number of lines from the top of a file scanned for Option Strict/Explicit and other header-required rules. Default: 40.
detection._comment_mvp_guard_lookback_lines: How many lines above an MVP index access are checked for a .Count guard (OIM571). Default: 10.
detection._comment_nearby_throw_lookahead_lines: Forward look-ahead window (lines) when checking whether a rethrow follows a caught exception. Default: 6.
detection._comment_nearby_dispose_lookahead_lines: Forward look-ahead window (lines) when checking whether a Using or Dispose follows resource creation. Default: 8.
detection._comment_context_lines: Default number of preceding lines examined for contextual checks. Default: 6.
detection._comment_global_max_examples_per_file: Fallback cap on duplicate-heavy rule examples per file when no per-rule max_examples_per_file is set. Default: 8.

confidence._comment: Thresholds used by Get-OneImAuditConfidenceLabel in OneimAudit.Common.psm1
confidence._comment_high_threshold: Minimum score (0-100) for a finding to be labelled High confidence. Default: 80.
confidence._comment_medium_threshold: Minimum score (0-100) for a finding to be labelled Medium confidence. Default: 60.

scoring._comment: Controls the per-category risk scoring section in the HTML report

severity_weights._comment: Multipliers applied to finding counts when computing the weighted risk score. Adjust to increase/decrease the penalty for high-severity findings.

category_thresholds._comment: Score thresholds (weighted findings per file) that define the colour band for each category card. Scores below green_max are green; between green_max and yellow_max are yellow; between yellow_max and orange_max are orange; above orange_max are red.

overall_thresholds._comment: Same colour bands but for the aggregate (sum of all categories) overall risk score.
